Let’s you and him fight, as the saying goes. While Israel watches. Quietly. One hears almost nothing about Israel all of a sudden. “Bibi” isn’t to be seen. Previously, it was hard not to see him. It’s almost as if this war doesn’t involve Israel when, of course, it is all about Israe
item.content:encoded: Features Politics Slide Show Facebook X Pinterest WhatsApp It is interesting that the country that goaded the United States – well, goaded Trump – into starting the war is no longer an active participant in the war. Let’s you and him fight, as the saying goes. While Israel watches. Quietly. One hears almost nothing about Israel all of a sudden. “Bibi” isn’t to be seen. Previously, it was hard not to see him. It’s almost as if this war doesn’t involve Israel when, of course, it is all about Israel. There would be no war were it not for Israel. Trump has admitted this, telling the world he had to attack Iran because Israel was going to. This somehow makes sense to Trump. More finely, he hopes it makes sense to Americans. The premise seems to be that had the United States not attacked Iran – had only Israel attacked Iran – somehow that would have resulted in harm to America. Well, that’s exactly what happened. Americans have been killed and something on the order of $100 billion has been lost – so far. Americans are paying $4 for gas (again) when they had been paying about $2.80 for it just six months ago, before the war began. Now America has assumed total responsibility for this stupid, evil war that was started by and for Israel. More finely, Americans have been saddled with total responsibility for this stupid, evil war that was started by and for Israel. Israel is (apparently, who knows given the near total suppression of actually reliable news) no longer being bombed and Israelis are no longer being killed but Americans are being killed and American facilities are being bombed. Mozel Tov, eh? Does anyone in this country notice? Does anyone care? Or are Americans so exhausted by it all they’ve become passive like the herd animals – stupid cattle – they’re looked upon by the people responsible for fomenting and perpetuating this war? The Iranians have refrained – so far – from bombing the country responsible for starting the war, which furthers the perception that it’s a war between the United States and Iran rather than a war for Israel being prosecuted (and paid-for) by the United States. It’s a brilliant because necessary development – from the Israeli perspective. Israel can pose as being a kind of Middle Eastern Switzerland while the United States bashes Iran and Iran bashes back. But is it sensible for the Iranians to allow themselves to be maneuvered into being the sole belligerent? The marketing campaign that is now under way is framing it that way. Israel – and Israel’s interests – recede from the news and so from Americans’ minds. It’s those Iranians who are causing us to have to spend $80 to fill up! This appears to be the hope, at any rate, in Washington and Tel Aviv. Maybe the Iranians ought to focus once more on the country that started this war – in order to end this war. Iran probably cannot do much to bring it home to Americans, except indirectly (via economic damage, such as $4 gas) and besides, if Iran did try to bring it home to America directly, it would only serve the interests of Israel by arousing the anger of Americans who are for the most part not especially angry at the Persians right now, because why would they be? They are certainly not angry enough to want this stupid, evil war – and the $4 gas that they’re paying for it. The anger – the righteous anger – ought to be directed at the instigators of this war. Not the Persians. That anger was reaching the boiling the point in this country. Not because Americans are “anti-Semitic” – oy vey – but because they are tired of their country being dragged in to stupid, evil wars for the sake of Israel. There is a distinction there. It is one Charlie Kirk had caught on to and was beginning to openly talk about. Now there is no more Charlie – but the catching-on has continued nonetheless. That is why something had to be done. That something was the Memorandum of Agreement that temporarily paused the war for just long enough to resume it without Israel. To reframe it as a war between Iran and the United States. To cause people to forget who started it and to get them out of it while Iran and the United States fight it for the benefit of the country and the interests that started it. It is important that Americans never forget how this war started – and why. . . . If you like what you’ve found here please consider supporting EPautos. We depend on you to keep the wheels turning! Our donate button is here. If you prefer not to use PayPal, our mailing address is: EPautos 721 Hummingbird Lane SE Copper Hill, VA 24079 PS: Get an EPautos magnet or sticker or coaster in return for a $25 or more one-time donation or a $10 or more monthly recurring donation. (Please be sure to tell us you want a magnet or sticker or coaster – and also, provide an address, so we know where to mail the thing!) If you’d like a Baaaaa hat or other EPautos gear, see here! Related posts: Israel is Silent Israel is not Our Ally . . . and Iran is not Our Enemy An Ominous Confession Orange Man Attacks War Tax Report: 07/18/2026

item.title: Faking “Water Bankruptcy” Part 2: Climate Change – “Later this year, the UN will be hosting a major summit on the world’s water supply. The Global Water Conference 2026 is being assembled to tackle wh
item.description: Jul 20, 2026 52 Faking “Water Bankruptcy” Part 2: Climate Change Kit Knightly Part one HERE Later this year, the UN will be hosting a major summit on the world’s water supply. The Global Water Conference 2026 is being assembled to tackle what the UN is calling “water bankruptcy”, and pursue Sustainable Development Goal 6 in pursuit of Agenda 2030. Let’s be clear here – we’re not talking about populous arid regions out-growing their aquifer capacity, or aging infrastructure lagging behind urban s
item.content:encoded: Jul 20, 2026 52 Faking “Water Bankruptcy” Part 2: Climate Change Kit Knightly Part one HERE Later this year, the UN will be hosting a major summit on the world’s water supply. The Global Water Conference 2026 is being assembled to tackle what the UN is calling “water bankruptcy”, and pursue Sustainable Development Goal 6 in pursuit of Agenda 2030. Let’s be clear here – we’re not talking about populous arid regions out-growing their aquifer capacity, or aging infrastructure lagging behind urban sprawl. All these are ongoing issues that need to be dealt with – but this is not what the Global Water Conference is aimed at. Rather it’s a focal point for a global “re-thinking” of the water supply, aimed at reaching all of us, even in regions with regular rainfall and plentiful fresh water. The narrative being sold is that the world wide water supply is under threat in some new and critical way, and drastic – potentially global – action is required. Another ‘crisis’ in other words. Another Hegelian Dialectic. And we know what those mean. In part one of this series, I discussed how the press and pet “experts” have recently been misrepresenting, exaggerating and simplifying the water usage of data centres, and the role this blatant propaganda plays in promoting the developing “water bankruptcy” narrative. But there’s a lot more to this narrative than just AI or data centres. The ‘threat’ to our global water supply is already multi-layered. And one of those layers is, inevitably, climate change. I have no intention of re-litigating so-called anthropogenic global warming (AGW) here. There are acres of pages on this subject. Suffice to say, AGW is a tentpole issue of the globalist world take-over. A creation of corrupt science and relentless propaganda that is used to excuse any-and-all tyrannical overreach a government can come up with. That climate change will lead to a supposed “water crisis” is not a new talking point; it goes back years – even decades – and is inextricably linked with the myriad of predicted disasters from acid rain to the hole in the Ozone layer to the disappearing ice caps. Media stories of droughts are endemic of course – some of them entirely genuine, others cherry-picked and exaggerated or manipulated to invoke AGW – but significantly the recurring theme in the most recent water panic stories is NOT that climate change will reduce the amount of water per se, but rather that it will make our existing water more dangerous to drink. Dangerous Waters As far as I can determine this narrative began emerging in late 2019 and early 2020, with articles like this one: Will climate change increase the presence of pathogens in drinking water? Over the course of the next few years they dropped the question mark and got a little more assertive: Climate change can worsen your drinking water quality These days, stories and papers on this issue just keep on coming. In February, the US Environmental Protection Agency published a report titled “Climate Change and Freshwater Harmful Algal Blooms”, which warns: Scientists continue to document many effects of climate change on freshwaters, estuaries, and marine environments, and those effects are predicted to be exacerbated in the future. Last Spring, the Yale School of the Environment published this paper: How Climate Change Puts the Safety of Drinking Water at Risk Which warns of climate change-related flooding and fires contaminating drinking water. Last July, the UN reported that melting glaciers could release prehistoric microbes into drinking water: Climate scientists are saying many glaciers will not survive this century unless action is taking to slow the melting rate caused by climate change. That means those living downstream will face a tide of floods alongside threats posed by reactivated microbes in a warming cryosphere or frozen parts of the Earth. Frozen in ice sheets, glaciers and permafrost are bacteria, fungi and viruses. While most are dead, some are dormant and some are active. As global temperatures hit record highs, these microorganisms will become more active in many ecosystems. Even if the melting can be slowed down by mitigating greenhouse gas emissions, efforts must assess and prepare for possible threats from potential pathogens. In January, Science Daily reported “urgent warnings” from Chinese scientists concerning the spread of amoebas in drinking water due, of course, to climate change: microscopic organisms are gaining ground worldwide, driven by climate change, deteriorating water systems, and limited monitoring and detection efforts. Just a couple of days ago, a new paper was reviewed by Phys.org, in an article titled: Climate change reshapes waterborne disease risks as pathogens respond differently In May, an article in the New Yorker headlined “Our Warming Planet Is a Petri Dish for New and Deadly Microbes”, warns that water may become increasingly unsafe as the planet warms, due to breeding microorganisms: “Flesh-eating” bacteria is spreading. Infectious fungi are emerging. Microbiomes may never be the same. Are we ready? Drinking water isn’t even the main thrust of the piece, but it’s in there. Setting the scene, quietly establishing the baseline. And the messaging is continuous. “There will come a time, in the near future, when climate change has rendered previously safe water dangerous.” They extend this ‘safe water becoming dangerous’ meme beyond climate change as well. New research tells us that a significant number of Americans have unsafe levels of nitrates in their water supply, or that “rainwater everywhere on earth is no longer safe” due to “forever chemicals”. You might argue the these are true statements to a point. Certainly levels of nitrates in drinking water have been of concern for several decades, since the rise of intensive farming. But since when did the controlled media give a solitary damn about that? And why – now – are they suddenly realising it matters? The coverage itself is an indication of narrative direction. They are discovering their concern about nitrates – because it is now convenient to do so. The common thread here is that – either through microbes, or contaminants – previously safe water may become somehow un-safe. That most over-used, emotive and politicized of words. The role of “Safety” “Safe” is a mighty word in the tyrannical control industry. Its definition is ever re-shaping, crowding on us or retreating over the horizon. What is “safe”? “Safe” in the modern sense used by media is a word defined from the top down, by regulatory bodies and international panels, and thus it affords them total narrative control. Its beauty and appeal to propagandists is its considered entirely essential by most people but is also entirely subjective, and therefore very useful when manipulating “reality.” This is true when applied to almost anything – disease, pollution, red meat…or drinking water. Everyone wants to be safe. And a lot of people will endure almost anything if the alternative is having their safety compromised. The “pandemic” showed us that scientific screening or testing can be used to control the speed and direction of a narrative. It utilized fake tests, administered according to official protocols, to not only create the pandemic in the first place, but then control the ebb and flow of “cases” and “deaths”, and the amount of “safety” people were permitted to experience. The more they “tested” the more danger they revealed. The more danger they revealed, the more public fear permitted them to legislate for the “threat”. I think the same model is being eased into place over the “water crisis”. I think it’s no coincidence that several of the papers I link to above declare the need for “increased surveillance” (testing) of the water supply. “Water screening” or “microbe surveillance” for amoebas or nitrates or whatever the scare chemical of the day is, can work the same way the PCR tests did, to create a ‘problem’ in need of solving. Lay the groundwork in the media with some propaganda to get people used to the idea things are changing and water might not be so safe any more. Then, when you’re prepared, start sending the EPA or DEFRA out to do some random fieldwork. And with no more effort than that, we are only a few “tests” or a simple adjustment of “recommended levels” away from suddenly having “dangerous” drinking water, that requires certain reservoirs (as many as you like) to be removed from circulation – until the water can be treated or “safe levels of X can be attained” (which will be any time you choose from now until perpetuity) And thus real water scarcity can be engineered out of nothing, higher bills explained as unavoidable, rationing just part of the good old new normal. * And so we have two pieces of the puzzle in place. On the one hand, the media are pushing simplistic and distorted information about the technology sector’s use of water. This misinformation is used to instil fear of potential shortages. On the other, climate alarmists are warning us that what little water we do have is becoming increasingly dangerous for us to drink. This could, in turn, be used to engineer local ‘crises’ on demand, following the ‘covid’ model. We have the building blocks of a major global “problem” being put in place, but what’s the endgame? To know that we need to look at the potential ‘solutions’ that will be proffered. We’ll discuss those in part three. Thanks for reading... You can help us keep doing what we do. Every little helps and is hugely appreciated. For other ways to donate, including direct-transfer bank details click HERE. Categories: latest, Water Bankruptcy

item.title: Chilling New Clues Challenge Suicide Claim In Los Alamos Lab Worker’s Death – “Fresh evidence recovered from the remote New Mexico forest where Los Alamos National Laboratory administrative assistant
item.description: “…including the vanishing of Steven Garcia, a nuclear contractor with top clearance.” One of these, I believe it was Garcia, was a security guard. I doubt the Chinese would snatch him. The problem with this theory is the victims are in such wide ranging fields. It’s like saying there have been mysterious deaths and vanishings in pro sports. GROK says: “Approximately 1.2 to 1.4 million Americans currently hold an active Top Secret (TS) or TS/SCI security clearance.This group is part of a larger p
item.content:encoded: “…including the vanishing of Steven Garcia, a nuclear contractor with top clearance.” One of these, I believe it was Garcia, was a security guard. I doubt the Chinese would snatch him. The problem with this theory is the victims are in such wide ranging fields. It’s like saying there have been mysterious deaths and vanishings in pro sports. GROK says: “Approximately 1.2 to 1.4 million Americans currently hold an active Top Secret (TS) or TS/SCI security clearance.This group is part of a larger population of roughly 5.1 million Americans who hold some form of active federal security clearance (including Confidential and Secret levels)” GROK didn’t say how many held those clearances in the past, like this retired general. I’m sorry folks, this is way too big a group to raise my concern about a dozen people dying or disappearing. Reply

item.title: Reviving the Old Cold War Racket Against Cuba – “The State Department just issued a report that is straight out of the old Cold War playbook. It states that Cuba’s communist regime is behind the democ
item.description: EMAIL President Trump and Secretary of State Marco Rubio are making me feel like I’m smack dab back in the middle of the old Cold War racket — the racket that succeeded in making the U.S. national-security state and its ever-growing army of “defense” contractors a permanent feature of American life. The State Department just issued a report that is straight out of the old Cold War playbook. It states that Cuba’s communist regime is behind the democratic socialist movements and radical leftist gr
item.content:encoded: EMAIL President Trump and Secretary of State Marco Rubio are making me feel like I’m smack dab back in the middle of the old Cold War racket — the racket that succeeded in making the U.S. national-security state and its ever-growing army of “defense” contractors a permanent feature of American life. The State Department just issued a report that is straight out of the old Cold War playbook. It states that Cuba’s communist regime is behind the democratic socialist movements and radical leftist groups here in America . Reinforcing the report, Rubio delivered a talk last week at a big event hosted by the State Department in which he stated that Cuba’s “sprawling intelligence and ideological network helped to build the far left in our country and in our hemisphere.” In other words, the Reds are coming to get us … again! Everyone needs to start looking under his bed for a Red … again! And Cuba, which is located only 90 miles away from American shores, is clearly the spearhead of the international communist conspiracy that is determined to take over America … again! Too bad Senator Joseph McCarthy is no longer here when we need him. First question for Rubio: Is the international communist conspiracy still centered in Moscow or has it moved to Beijing? Second question for Rubio: Is socialist-communist Cuba also responsible for Rubio’s fervent belief in Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, public (i.e., government) schooling, and other socialist programs here in the United States? The Cold War racket was the best thing that ever happened to the Pentagon, the CIA, and the NSA, the three principal components of the U.S. national-security state. By striking deep fear of the Reds within the American people after World War II, warfare statists succeeded in converting the federal government from our founding system of a limited-government republic to a national-security state, which is a totalitarian-like governmental structure — and they did it without even the semblance of a constitutional amendment. That’s how Americans ended up living under omnipotent government — that is, government that wields the power to assassinate, torture, and incarcerate them for life without due process of law and trial by jury. It’s also how Americans have come to live under forever war and never-ending crises. It’s also how Americans live like little serfs under a massive, all-powerful, and very expensive military-intelligence establishment and its ever-growing army of voracious “defense” contractors. The report also mentions Lee Harvey Oswald, the supposed assassin of President Kennedy who denied his guilt and contended that he had been set up as a “patsy” to take the fall for the assassination. Not surprisingly, however, the report fails to mention that several months before his assassination, Kennedy achieved a “breakthrough” that enabled him to see that the Cold War was nothing but a deadly and destructive racket that was bringing America perilously close to all-out nuclear war. That’s why JFK was determined to end the Cold War racket and, in the process, to use his words, “splinter the CIA into a thousand pieces and scatter it to the winds.” Needless to say, the report also fails to point out that that Kennedy’s resolution of the Cuban Missile Crisis, which left Cuba permanently under communist control, his Peace Speech at American University, where he effectively announced an end of the Cold War racket, and his peace overtures to Cuba, which were occurring at the moment he was assassinated, were the principal reasons that the national-security establishment determined that JFK had become a grave threat to “national security” and, therefore, had to be eliminated. What Rubio’s report also fails to mention is that it has always been the U.S. government, specifically the national-security branch of the government, that has been the aggressor against Cuba, not the other way around. Don’t forget the U.S. government’s invasion of Cuba at the Bay of Pigs (as well as the lies that the CIA intentionally used against President Kennedy to garner his support for the invasion). Don’t forget Operation Mongoose, which entailed violent acts of sabotage against Cuban economic enterprises. Luis Posada Carriles Don’t forget the CIA’s assassination partnership with the Mafia, the world’s premier criminal organization, to murder Cuban leader Fidel Castro, a partnership that was later turned inward against Kennedy. Don’t forget CIA terrorist Luis Posada Carriles’s bombing of a Cuban airliner that killed the members of Cuba’s fencing team. And don’t forget his bombing campaign against Cuban hotels that killed more innocent people. The CIA, of course, has long claimed that Posada no longer was working for the CIA when he committed those horrific acts of murder. If you believe that, I’ve got a bridge in the Arizona desert I’d like to sell to you. After all, why then did U.S. officials protect Posada from being extradited to Venezuela to be tried and punished for his act of terrorism against the innocent Cubans in that airliner? Moreover, let’s not forget the decades-old Cold War economic embargo, which targeted — and continues to target — the Cuban people with death by starvation and illness. Is there any difference between dying from a plane crash or a terrorist attack on a hotel and dying from starvation or illness from an economic embargo? In either case, isn’t the person still dead? I’ll never forget what Cuban leader Che Guevara said when he was asked, soon after the Cuban revolution, what Cuba wanted from the United States. He replied: “Just leave us alone.” But the U.S. government has never been able to leave Cuba alone. The Pentagon-CIA-NSA quest for regime change in Cuba has been a decades-long obsession, one that clearly did not end with the end of the Cold War. U.S. officials are still determined to oust Cuba’s communist regime and replace it with another brutal right-wing dictatorship, like that of Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet, or like that of Fulgencio Batista, the U.S.-supported crooked and corrupt Cuban dictator who Castro ousted from power. It might be worth mentioning that Batista had a policy of kidnapping underaged Cuban girls and delivering them to his Mafia goons to serve as sexual perks for the well-heeled gamblers in the Mafia’s casinos in Havana. It might also be worth pointing out that Rubio’s parents did not leave Cuba after the communists took over, as he used to maintain, but instead left when the U.S. puppet Batista was still in power. The fact is that in the long, sordid relationship between Cuba and the United States, the U.S. government has always been the aggressor and the terrorist. Unfortunately, that continues to be the case today, as demonstrated not only by the decades-long U.S. economic embargo against the Cuban people but also now by a brutal and ruthless oil blockade designed to kill even more Cubans. In fact, it’s rather obvious that Trump and Rubio are reviving the old Cold War racket about the Reds coming to get us in order to prepare the American people for another invasion of Cuba, assuming, of course, they can first extricate themselves from their illegal and unconstitutional war of aggression against Iran. EMAIL

item.title: Trump Is NOT A Reluctant War President – “One of the dumbest popular narratives you see today is the idea that Zionists persuaded a reluctant Trump to start a war with Iran after he was re-elected, li
item.description: Reading by Tim Foley: America’s latest forever war continues to ravage the middle east as US forces bomb Iran for the tenth consecutive night in a series of exchanges which The New York Times reports have caused a significantly higher number of injuries to US troops than the Pentagon has acknowledged. President Trump is saying that “Every time Iran kills an American Soldier they will pay for that killing many times over” while simultaneously guaranteeing more Iranian retaliations with the ongoin
item.content:encoded: Reading by Tim Foley: America’s latest forever war continues to ravage the middle east as US forces bomb Iran for the tenth consecutive night in a series of exchanges which The New York Times reports have caused a significantly higher number of injuries to US troops than the Pentagon has acknowledged. President Trump is saying that “Every time Iran kills an American Soldier they will pay for that killing many times over” while simultaneously guaranteeing more Iranian retaliations with the ongoing onslaught, which could of course easily end up killing more US troops. Amid these escalating hostilities, Yemeni forces have announced a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia as the ceasefire the two nations have maintained since 2022 hurtles toward collapse. Trump inherited a ceasefire in the horrific war between Yemen and Saudi Arabia and seems to be flushing it right down the toilet, just like he inherited the Iran deal and torched it so he could start a war. He is a disgusting, evil war pig. Yemen's Houthis said they will impose a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia, threatening Red Sea oil shipments as global energy flows are already restricted by Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz https://t.co/gcwwZhBk3C pic.twitter.com/MxdozcGgsO — Reuters (@Reuters) July 20, 2026 One of the dumbest popular narratives you see today is the idea that Zionists persuaded a reluctant Trump to start a war with Iran after he was re-elected, like they were chasing him around going “Would you like to bomb Iran?” like the guy from Green Eggs and Ham while he yelled “Not in a box! Not with a fox!” until he eventually caved in. It is now abundantly clear that this war was planned a long time ago. Not in 2026, not in 2025, not in 2024, and not even during the Biden administration. This was planned before Trump was ever elected president. He campaigned on tearing up the Iran nuclear deal, and that’s exactly what he did once he got into office the first time around. As soon as the JCPOA was torched in 2018 the Trump administration began dramatically ramping up aggressions against Iran, smashing them with starvation sanctions in its “maximum pressure” campaign with the explicit goal of regime change and then escalating to the direct assassination of Iran’s top military official. There was no reason to do that if your goal was peace. There was no reason to do that if your goal was to prevent Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. The Iran deal was working. Iran was fully compliant. The escalations were the goal. Take note of which Trump comments provoke controversy, and which don't. Trump said this week that he "gave" the Golan Heights to Sheldon and Miriam Adelson, his top funders, who came to the White House "almost more than anybody." Not a peep about this brazen admission of graft pic.twitter.com/MaJLFnH7oi — Michael Tracey (@mtracey) September 22, 2024 Trump has openly admitted to giving the Adelsons everything they wanted in exchange for the massive campaign donations which put him in the White House. In 2013 Sheldon Adelson made headlines by saying the US should drop a nuclear bomb on the Iranian desert and then say “See? The next one is in the middle of Tehran” in order to scare the Iranians into groveling. Everything we’ve been seeing throughout Trump’s second term makes it glaringly obvious that the only reason he’d received the oligarchic and institutional support necessary to become an American president was because he had provided enough powerful people with enough reassurance that he would do what no other president had dared to try: start a war with Iran. All that we have seen throughout 2026 has been the seeds of those early assurances coming to fruition. All those murdered schoolchildren. All the dead and injured US soldiers. The bombed-out civilian infrastructure. The blackened skies. The blockades. The fear. The economic strife. This was all planned years ago, with the full complicity of a psychopathic monster who wanted to become president. He campaigned on ending the wars, and he was lying the whole time. He wasn’t pressured and persuaded, he was an active and eager participant. Every time Iran kills an American Soldier they will pay for that killing many times over. pic.twitter.com/KJ203VKate — The White House (@WhiteHouse) July 20, 2026 Anti-interventionists on the right often try to spin it as Trump getting suckered by the Israelis, because this allows them to retroactively justify their support for Trump with the belief that they voted for someone who was well-intentioned at the time. But Trump was never well-intentioned. He had always intended to start a war with Iran in his second term, once he no longer had to worry about getting re-elected. He was always a deceitful war slut, as evil as any we’ve ever seen in the White House. Evil people are the only ones who are ever allowed past the many gatekeeping mechanisms to the presidency. In order to help run a murderous empire that is fueled by human blood, you’ve got to be a psychopath. They don’t let you in unless they’ve been adequately assured that you are sufficiently psychopathic to do the job. Trump provided them with those assurances. That’s the only reason he sits where he now sits. ________________ The best way to make sure you see everything I publish is to get on my free mailing list. My work is entirely reader-supported, so if you enjoyed this piece here are some options where you can toss some money into my tip jar if you want to. Click here for links for my social media, books, merch, and audio/video versions of each article. All my work is free to bootleg and use in any way, shape or form; republish it, translate it, use it on merchandise; whatever you want. All works co-authored with my husband Tim Foley. Bitcoin donations: 1Ac7PCQXoQoLA9Sh8fhAgiU3PHA2EX5Zm2 Share this: Share on X (Opens in new window) X Share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Facebook Like Loading…

item.title: Oil hits $100 a barrel after Iran-backed Houthis ‘strike two tankers’ in Red Sea – “Attacks by proxy militia threaten to open new front in Iran war near alternative shipping route to Strait of Hormuz”
Governments Have No Authority Over Our God-Given Rights – “Fake ‘democracies’ cannot be allowed to take what God gives us.” – J.B. Shurk |
item.description: Advertisement Advertisement Politics > Democracy Link copied Governments Have No Authority Over Our God-Given Rights Fake ‘democracies’ cannot be allowed to take what God gives us. J.B. Shurk | July 23, 2026 pexels pexels Getting your Trinity Audio player ready... We Westerners have an important job to do. In the years and decades ahead, we must reassert our God-given rights. Advertisement Note that I do not assign this task to Western governments. Governments, in the best of times, are an annoy
item.content:encoded: Advertisement Advertisement Politics > Democracy Link copied Governments Have No Authority Over Our God-Given Rights Fake ‘democracies’ cannot be allowed to take what God gives us. J.B. Shurk | July 23, 2026 pexels pexels Getting your Trinity Audio player ready... We Westerners have an important job to do. In the years and decades ahead, we must reassert our God-given rights. Advertisement Note that I do not assign this task to Western governments. Governments, in the best of times, are an annoyance; in anything but the best of times, governments are oppressive and dangerous. It is in governments’ nature to use their respective monopolies on violence to take more and more from the citizens who live within their jurisdictions. It is unnatural for governments to fight for our rights and liberties because our rights and liberties are fundamental checks on every government’s powers. Governments will not fight for your free speech; they fight to be the only voices that can be heard. Governments will not fight for your religious freedom because governments cannot countenance a higher moral authority than their own. Governments will not fight for your property rights because their taxes and regulations rely on the immoral premise that your property is never really yours. Governments do not defend citizens’ natural, inalienable, God-given rights from attacks because governments are the offensive entities doing the attacking. Advertisement Only the people can defend their God-given rights. And only the people can decide when they’ve endured a sufficient number of attacks on their rights to stand up and tell their governments, “No more.” Until citizens do so, governments will pretend that the people have given them permission to infringe upon those natural rights and accrue more power for themselves. Across the West, governments continue to probe just how far they can go before the people push back. They regulate what citizens are permitted to say on social media platforms. They censor points of view that contradict official government narratives. They criminalize public expressions of Christian faith. They read our emails, bank records, medical charts, chat messages, credit card statements, phone logs, and online shopping histories without first obtaining warrants or establishing probable cause that any crimes have been committed. They spy on us with their cameras, computers, AI systems, and drones. Advertisement Governments violate our God-given rights every single day, while telling us that these violations are justified. They tell us that their attacks on our speech, faith, privacy, and domestic sovereignty are done for our own good. That’s a bit like spousal abuse, is it not? Just as a bad husband might beat his wife until she “learns to behave,” governments censor, demean, spy on, and threaten us into submission. Whether through taxation, regulation, police intimidation, constant monitoring, or criminal enforcement, every government creates the conditions for an abusive relationship. The only question is how abusive each government will become. A major reason why our rights and liberties are under attack today is that so many of us in the West have been asleep. We told ourselves that we belonged to the nations that defeated fascism, Nazism, and, more generally, totalitarianism. We spent decades standing up to the Soviet Union and championing freedom over communist tyranny. We took pride in knowing that we lived in countries where you could speak your mind without going to jail. We understood that in despotic nations, such as communist China, Christians practice their faith in secret. We saw images of tanks massacring Chinese demonstrators in Tiananmen Square. We heard stories of famine and mass execution coming from North Korea. We knew that communists and drug cartels slaughtered people in South and Central America. Knowing that extreme barbarity and oppression exist elsewhere in the world made us feel relatively comfortable and safe. In other words, we took our freedoms for granted. Advertisement We forgot that totalitarianism is not some exotic creation of the Italians, Germans, or Japanese. We allowed ourselves to believe that the forms of government oppression against which our family members fought in prior wars could never find a foothold here. But just because our nations fought oppression in the past does not mean our governments became naturally immune to the totalitarian disease. In some ways, Western governments have found it easier to imprison us precisely because we celebrated our past successes while ignoring the signs of failure in our own countries. We told ourselves, “We have free speech.” Our governments said, “Sure you do, except for words and ideas that we designate as ‘dis-,’ ‘mal-,’ or ‘mis-information,’” and we deluded ourselves into thinking that that contradiction somehow made sense. We told ourselves, “We are free to practice our religious faiths without fear of government interference.” Our governments said, “Sure you are, except if you choose to pray before a football game or outside of an abortion clinic, and, of course, you definitely can’t call homosexuality a sin or ‘transgenderism’ a mental disorder”; and we convinced ourselves that governments’ will took precedence over God’s will. We told ourselves, “Our homes, papers, private communications, and personal effects are secure against government searches and seizures.” Our governments said, “Of course they are, but we’re still going to have to look through all of your personal messages and belongings to make sure that you’re not doing anything illegal or spreading ‘disinformation’ or engaging in nondescript forms of ‘hate’”; and we assured ourselves that our governments were only spying on us in order to “protect the children” from all manner of threats. Advertisement All the while, these governments that we defended as part of the “free world” have adopted elements of the closed, authoritarian, centrally-controlled systems that leaders of the “free world” claim to oppose. Western officials never miss an opportunity to rail against “fascism,” while working hand-in-glove with unelected central banks, NGOs, multinational corporations, and vast armies of bureaucrats to impose the will of a small cabal of elites upon entire nations of citizens. Western officials rarely say anything negative about communism because they are constructing socialist systems that share much in common with the Chinese Communist Party. Western officials have spent ten years telling us that the election of President Donald Trump in the United States represents a resurgence in “authoritarianism,” yet those same officials demand that Westerners obey the authority of so-called “experts” and the edicts that come from international organizations, “climate change” treaties, and health bureaucracies. Western governments engage in all these contradictions by playing a devilish magic trick against Western peoples: They never speak about rights or freedoms. They speak only of “democracy.” Advertisement “Democracy,” however, means nothing without a Bill of Rights or similar charter that effectively prevents governments from infringing the natural rights of the people. In the United Kingdom, the unelected king just made Andy Burnham prime minister, even though the former mayor of Greater Manchester was handed a seat in Parliament. In Ukraine, dictator Volodymyr Zelenskyy remains “president” two years after the expiration of his term. In France, the Netherlands, and Germany, prosecutors, courts, intelligence agencies, and news media conspire to prevent politicians who believe in national sovereignty from coming to power. In Romania, a presidential election was canceled when the wrong candidate won (who was then prosecuted for made-up “crimes” as so many other pro-nation politicians have been throughout the West). Unelected European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen controls the legislation and foreign policy of the continent. In the United States, President Obama’s administration framed President Trump as a Russian spy; Democrats rigged the 2020 “election” with unlawful mail-in-ballots; and unethical prosecutors and judges fabricated charges against Trump that could have imprisoned him for life. Then, in the 2024 presidential contest, Democrats summarily replaced Joe Biden with Kamala Harris, even though she never won a single primary. This is what Western governments call “democracy.” As Western governments more frequently resort to installing leaders at whim, it becomes more difficult to distinguish Western “democracy” from oligarchy or dictatorship. This is why we must reassert our God-given rights. Fake “democracies” cannot take what only God can give us. Image via Pexels. Related Topics: Democracy View & Add Comments SUPPORT AMERICAN THINKER Now more than ever, the ability to speak our minds is crucial to the republic we cherish. If what you see on American Thinker resonates with you, please consider supporting our work with a donation of as much or as little as you can give. Every dollar contributed helps us pay our staff and keep our ideas heard and our voices strong. Thank you. item.title: The New Energy Map: Power, Technology, and the Architecture of the Twenty-First Century – “Energy is no longer simply a commodity — it has become the operating system of global power, where oil, elect
item.description: Economy The New Energy Map: Power, Technology, and the Architecture of the Twenty-First Century Phil Butler, July 22, 2026 Energy is no longer simply a commodity — it has become the operating system of global power, where oil, electricity, semiconductors, and artificial intelligence are woven into a single architecture redefining the balance of power in the 21st century. For more than a century, geopolitical power was measured in barrels of oil, miles of pipeline, and fleets of tankers. Nations
item.content:encoded: Economy The New Energy Map: Power, Technology, and the Architecture of the Twenty-First Century Phil Butler, July 22, 2026 Energy is no longer simply a commodity — it has become the operating system of global power, where oil, electricity, semiconductors, and artificial intelligence are woven into a single architecture redefining the balance of power in the 21st century. For more than a century, geopolitical power was measured in barrels of oil, miles of pipeline, and fleets of tankers. Nations rose and fell according to their access to hydrocarbons, while wars and economic fortunes were determined by the ability to secure reliable energy supplies. The twentieth century belonged to those who controlled the fuel that powered industry and military might. That equation is now changing. Oil and natural gas remain indispensable, but they no longer define energy power alone. The twenty-first century has produced a sophisticated equation in which hydrocarbons coexist with electricity, nuclear power, artificial intelligence, semiconductors, critical minerals, and advanced manufacturing. The evolution of the energy dynamic does not rely on replacing one system with another. The world is constructing an interconnected architecture where each nation in the chain depends upon the others. The emerging energy order cannot be understood through any single commodity or nation Rather than eliminating geopolitics, the race toward decarbonization has multiplied the number of strategic assets nations must secure. Every battery factory requires lithium and cobalt; every AI data center demands enormous electricity; every electric vehicle depends on semiconductor ecosystems spanning continents. Energy has ceased to be merely an economic commodity. It has become the architectural core upon which technological leadership, industrial competitiveness, and economic resilience depend. The New Rules of Energy Power The old geopolitical formula was elegantly simple: control oil, control power. Today’s equation is considerably more complicated. Modern energy security depends on a nation’s ability to integrate multiple systems simultaneously. Hydrocarbon resources remain essential, but so do electricity grids capable of supporting digital economies. Manufacturing capacity has become as strategically important as resource extraction. And some nations are excelling in this area. The countries shaping tomorrow’s energy order are not necessarily those possessing the largest reserves beneath their soil. They are those capable of combining natural resources, industrial production, technological innovation, and logistics into resilient national ecosystems. Pipelines, LNG terminals, and oil fields still matter, but none guarantees geopolitical influence alone. Energy has evolved from a commodity into an operating system linking nearly every dimension of modern civilization. Resource Power Under Pressure: Russia and the Enduring Importance of Hydrocarbons Since 2022, successive sanctions and Europe’s rapid reduction of Russian gas imports were expected to permanently diminish Moscow’s energy influence. Reality has proven considerably more nuanced. Rather than disappearing from global commerce, Russian energy exports largely changed direction. As we have seen, China and India dramatically increased purchases of discounted crude, while alternative shipping and new payment mechanisms reduced dependence on Western finance. Moscow accelerated cooperation with BRICS members, expanded Eurasian pipeline diplomacy, and continued investing in Arctic shipping routes. These adaptations have not been painless. Replacing highly profitable European markets requires accepting lower prices and higher logistical costs, while sanctions complicate Arctic development. Yet Russia retains strategic advantages few nations can replicate. It remains a leading producer of oil, gas, uranium, and electricity, possessing enormous reserves and a geography that allows it to redirect commerce between Europe and Asia. The broader lesson is that energy systems possess remarkable resilience. Political decisions can redirect trade, but geography and resource abundance remain powerful forces that markets continually accommodate. The result is not the disappearance of energy power, but its reconfiguration and, in some cases, magnified importance. Manufacturing the Future: China and the Industrialization of the Energy Transition If Russia demonstrates the enduring importance of natural resources, China illustrates the growing importance of everything that happens after those resources leave the ground. China did not simply build factories; it built ecosystems. Long before renewable energy dominated political debate, Beijing recognized that manufacturing the technologies of tomorrow could prove as strategically valuable as controlling the oil fields of yesterday. Chinese planners approached solar panels, batteries, electric vehicles, and rare earth processing not as separate industries, but as interconnected components of a comprehensive industrial strategy. Today, China dominates global solar manufacturing, battery production, EV output, and rare earth processing. It continues expanding nuclear generation and ultra-high-voltage transmission while investing heavily in advanced research. These achievements resulted from decades of coordinated industrial policy emphasizing scale and long-term planning. Through the Belt and Road Initiative, Chinese firms have financed infrastructure across Asia, Africa, and Latin America, strengthening Beijing’s position throughout the supply chains underpinning the global energy transition. China’s greatest strategic achievement is its understanding that manufacturing itself constitutes geopolitical power. While many countries debated climate targets, Beijing constructed the industrial capacity to supply the world with the technologies enabling that transition. Consequently, nations pursuing energy independence remain simultaneously dependent upon Chinese factories and industriousness. Despite relying heavily on imported raw materials and facing demographic pressures, China’s manufacturing ecosystems ensure it will remain a central architect of the evolving energy order. America’s Strategic Crossroads: Innovation, LNG, and the Digital Economy If China is the manufacturing center of the energy transition, the United States remains its principal engine of technological innovation. The shale revolution transformed the U.S. into a leading oil and gas producer, while rapid LNG expansion has reshaped markets and provided Washington with new strategic leverage, particularly as Europe sought alternatives to Russian gas. Yet hydrocarbons represent only one pillar of American strength. The U.S. continues to dominate venture capital, semiconductor design, software, aerospace, AI, and the financial institutions allocating global capital. Increasingly, these sectors converge around one common denominator: energy. Artificial intelligence has transformed electricity from a utility into a strategic resource. Every hyperscale data center requires enormous power, while semiconductor fabs demand electrical stability once associated with military infrastructure. The next generation of economic competition will depend not merely on computing power, but on the energy systems capable of sustaining it. The U.S. combines abundant resources with technological leadership and deep capital markets. However, its future LNG dominance is not guaranteed; market conditions, competing global developments, and the pace of the energy transition will determine its commercial and geopolitical influence. Domestically, aging transmission networks, grid fragmentation, and lengthy permitting processes threaten to outpace investment. America’s next phase of leadership will depend less on discovering new resources than on modernizing the infrastructure required to deliver them. Two Roads to the Same Future: Europe and the Gulf Few regions illustrate the complexity of today’s energy transformation more clearly than Europe and the Gulf states. Their starting points differ vastly, yet both are attempting to solve the same strategic problem: how to remain prosperous during a profound structural shift. Now, the conflicts in the region signal a paradigm shift with regard to the U.S. role in the region. Iran’s unexpected resilience and Israel’s geopolitical status nightmare will certainly reshape the geopolitics and energy fields in the region. Europe has pursued an ambitious decarbonization strategy through the European Green Deal and aggressive emissions targets. However, the disruption of Russian energy supplies exposed the vulnerabilities of transforming an energy system while simultaneously replacing a major supplier. Electricity prices surged, and heavy industries faced unprecedented pressure as costs diverged from competitors in North America and Asia. Europe’s experience proves that climate leadership alone does not guarantee economic resilience. The transition requires enormous investment in infrastructure and storage to preserve competitiveness. The Gulf states confront an almost opposite challenge. Rather than reducing dependence on hydrocarbons, countries like Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar are using today’s energy revenues to finance tomorrow’s economies. Massive investments in AI, hydrogen, logistics, and advanced manufacturing reflect a recognition that hydrocarbon wealth must ultimately become diversified economic strength. This is not a retreat from oil and gas, but an attempt to ensure those revenues remain relevant by financing entirely new sectors. Both regions acknowledge that future competitiveness depends on adaptation rather than inertia. The Next Resource Frontier: Africa and Latin America Every previous energy revolution created new strategic geographies. Today’s transition is shifting attention toward Africa and Latin America, where critical minerals essential to electrification are concentrated. Copper, lithium, cobalt, and rare earth elements are indispensable to batteries, renewables, and advanced electronics. Countries like Chile, Zambia, and the DRC possess extraordinary mineral wealth capable of shaping global supply chains. History, however, offers a cautionary lesson: natural resources alone rarely produce lasting prosperity. The greatest economic value increasingly lies in processing, manufacturing, and industrial integration. This reality has intensified strategic competition among major powers seeking secure access to these materials. For resource-rich developing nations, the opportunity is historic. If accompanied by industrial investment and technological capability, critical minerals could support broader economic transformation. Without those complementary investments, many risk repeating familiar patterns where raw materials are exported while higher-value manufacturing occurs elsewhere. The next great energy competition may concern not who owns the minerals but who captures the greatest share of the value they create. The Architecture of the New Energy Order Every major energy revolution has reshaped the geopolitical landscape. The current transformation differs because it is occurring across multiple systems simultaneously. Hydrocarbons remain indispensable even as renewables expand. AI dramatically increases electricity demand while nations pursue decarbonization. Critical minerals have become as strategically significant as pipelines. The emerging energy order cannot be understood through any single commodity or nation. Russia demonstrates the resilience of natural resources under pressure. China illustrates how manufacturing ecosystems become instruments of influence. The U.S. highlights the convergence of energy, technology, and AI. Europe reveals the economic challenges of large-scale transformation, while the Gulf leverages hydrocarbon wealth to secure the future. Africa and Latin America hold the mineral resources upon which every major economy depends. No nation today controls every element of the emerging system. Instead, the twenty-first century is producing an integrated architecture in which resources, manufacturing, electricity, computation, and finance are inseparable components of national power. The countries most likely to shape the coming decades will be those capable of integrating these assets into resilient ecosystems that can adapt to rapidly changing realities. Energy is no longer simply the fuel of civilization. It has become its operating system. And the nations that understand this transformation first are unlikely merely to compete within the new global order. They will help build it. Phil Butler is a policy investigator and analyst, a political scientist and expert on Eastern Europe, and an author of the recent bestseller “Putin’s Praetorians” and other books Follow new articles on our Telegram channel More on this topic The Middle East’s Great Strategic Realignment Has Begun Abbas Hashemite The Guardian Myth: Why America’s Hormuz Toll Plan Is Already Sinking Salman Rafi Sheikh The Hormuz Extortion: How Washington’s Maritime Toll Weaponizes Global Trade for Big Oil Phil Butler Riyadh and the Canal projects to bypass the Strait of Hormuz, obstacles, and prospects Samyar Rostami Why Iran and the US are fighting over Hormuz — Again Salman Rafi Sheikh Tags: Energy carriers, Energy resources, geoeconomics, Multipolar world Network edition New Eastern Outlook 2010-2026 Republishing of the articles is welcomed with reference to NEO. The views of the authors do not necessarily coincide with the opinion of the editorial board.

item.title: How Much is it Going to Cost Us? – “How much could Trump’s stupid, evil – and apparently never-ending – war with Iran ( for Israel) cost us? it could soon be costing Americans $7 per gallon – due to t
item.description: Features Politics Slide Show Facebook X Pinterest WhatsApp How much could Trump’s stupid, evil – and apparently never-ending – war with Iran (for Israel) cost us? According to Rapidan Energy Group, which forecasts global oil and gas supply/price trends, it could soon be costing Americans $7 per gallon – due to the price of crude oil reaching (possibly surpassing) $200 per barrel. “Hormuz disruptions will be deeper and more persistent than we previously expected, materially tightening global oil
item.content:encoded: Features Politics Slide Show Facebook X Pinterest WhatsApp How much could Trump’s stupid, evil – and apparently never-ending – war with Iran (for Israel) cost us? According to Rapidan Energy Group, which forecasts global oil and gas supply/price trends, it could soon be costing Americans $7 per gallon – due to the price of crude oil reaching (possibly surpassing) $200 per barrel. “Hormuz disruptions will be deeper and more persistent than we previously expected, materially tightening global oil balances through 2027,” says a statement issued by Rapidan. “Expect ongoing military risks to constrain vessel transits through the Strait well into next year, keeping Arab Gulf production below pre-conflict levels through 4Q27.” Italics added. Did you get that? Rapidan is predicting much higher oil (and so, gas) prices through the fourth quarter of 2027. As in all year, next year. Probably beginning this year. It does not take a prophet to predict this because it’s already happening. As of July 23 – the date this column was written – the cost of gasoline had already risen about 25 cents per gallon over the course of a little more than a week. It is now – according to AAA – $4.02 per gallon. It is much more in some areas. If the war does not end soon – more precisely, if the Strait isn’t opened soon – it is a near-certainty that it will continue to rise. The only question at this point is how high can it go? Rapidan predicts it could go ballistic. The thing about ballistic things, of course, is that after they go up they always come down. In this case, everything could go down in tandem. People can’t buy what they can’t afford. When that happens at scale, everything goes down. Does Trump give any thought to the consequences of gasoline at $7 per gallon? Probably not, because gasoline could go up to $20 per gallon and he wouldn’t feel it. None of the creatures responsible for this stupid, evil war for Israel have to worry about feeling it. They have the means of Crassus – combined with the ethics of Crassus. Of course, Crassus didn’t end so well. If Trump read books he might have read about that. Seven dollar gas would end America as we have known it. It would “break the back” of the world economy, in the word’s of Rapidan’s CEO, Bob McNally. Suburbs would become economically untenable places for most of the people who currently live there to continue living there because most of the people living there have to drive to work and if gasoline goes up to $7 per gallon, it will no longer be economically tenable to continue driving to work. Filling up a typical crossover SUV such as a Toyota RAV4 – which takes about 15 gallons of gas – would cost more than $100. A full-size SUV or truck that takes 22-24 gallons would cost $150-170. Perhaps by itself this could be managed by cutting back on other things but most people have already cut back on most other things. Rapidan doesn’t mention the elephant in the room – which is diesel fuel. If the price of gas goes up to $7 per gallon, the price of diesel will go up to $10 per gallon and that will make food a luxury for tens of millions of people. Forget about filling the tank. Diesel is the invisible fuel of the American (the world) economy. It is not gasoline that powers the big rigs, the trains – and even the ships – that bring food and the things needed to produce food from Here to There. It is diesel. The opening monologue of the Road Warrior had it wrong. The world didn’t crumble because gazzuline became scarce. Well, it did – in the movie. In reality, it will crumble when diesel becomes scarce (or unaffordable, which amounts to the same thing). It’s all horrible to contemplate because it’s a nightmare scenario of suffering for billions of people. It not just the American people who will suffer as a result of Trump’s stupid, evil war The entire world is going to suffer. This would be horrible just because it’s horrible – but it is something much worse because it is engineered horribleness. None of this had to happen. It was made to happen. Trump chose to wage war on Iran when he could have chosen not to. The Iranians had not attacked us. They had annoyed Israel. Somehow, annoying Israel justifies the United States attacking Iran. Well, it justified attacking Iran – to Trump. And why not? It’s not as if his material (or physical) well-being is on the line. It’s ours that is – and for a sociopath or psychopath, that is a matter of indifference. These people not only don’t care about us they don’t have to care about us. This point is critical to understanding why this stupid, evil war began and why it isn’t ending. The people who started it don’t care about the consequences because there are none for them. Not the kind that are going to befall us, at any rate. They will not starve. They will not lose their homes. They will not even run out of gas – because these creatures will always have access to the things (including the necessities) they take from us or which their stupid, evil actions deny to us. As the war grinds on, these consequences grow more certain and ominous. Just not for them. . . . If you like what you’ve found here please consider supporting EPautos. We depend on you to keep the wheels turning! Our donate button is here. If you prefer not to use PayPal, our mailing address is: EPautos 721 Hummingbird Lane SE Copper Hill, VA 24079 PS: Get an EPautos magnet or sticker or coaster in return for a $25 or more one-time donation or a $10 or more monthly recurring donation. (Please be sure to tell us you want a magnet or sticker or coaster – and also, provide an address, so we know where to mail the thing!) If you’d like a Baaaaa hat or other EPautos gear, see here! Related posts: Where’s Israel? Gas Doesn’t Cost More . . . Our Dollars Are Worth Less Israel is Silent An Ominous Confession Will We Reminisce About $4.30 Per Gallon Gas?

item.title: Von der Leyen paints target signs on Europe with Ukraine drone pact – “This is not sleepwalking into war. The European elites’ eyes are wide open and crazed.” – Strategic Culture Foundation
item.description: Editorial Von der Leyen paints target signs on Europe with Ukraine drone pact July 17, 2026 © Photo: SCF This is not sleepwalking into war. The European elites’ eyes are wide open and crazed. Join us on Telegram, X, and VK. Contact us: info@strategic-culture.su The European Union has officially joined the war effort against Russia. This week, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen signed a “historic pact” with Ukraine to integrate the production of combat drones to strike Russia. Acc
item.content:encoded: Editorial Von der Leyen paints target signs on Europe with Ukraine drone pact July 17, 2026 © Photo: SCF This is not sleepwalking into war. The European elites’ eyes are wide open and crazed. Join us on Telegram, X, and VK. Contact us: info@strategic-culture.su The European Union has officially joined the war effort against Russia. This week, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen signed a “historic pact” with Ukraine to integrate the production of combat drones to strike Russia. According to Euronews reporting, the pact also envisages the joint production of ballistic missiles. This scaled-up war effort will be funded by the €90 billion loan that the EU is releasing to Ukraine. A bill that will be paid for by generations of European taxpayers. Von der Leyen was greeted in Kiev by Ukraine’s unelected martial-law president, Vladimir Zelensky. That makes two people who do not have a democratic mandate making decisions that are driving Europe to all-out war with Russia. Von der Leyen is a political appointee. She is not elected by popular ballot. Formerly, she was Germany’s Minister of Defense, and not a particularly bright one either. Dogged by corruption scandals and abuse of power over procuring Covid-19 vaccines from Big Pharma, von der Leyen is an exemplar of “failing upwards.” On her 11th visit to Kiev since the conflict escalated in February 2022, von der Leyen declared that the “tide is turning” in the NATO proxy war against Russia. She was referring to the increase in long-range air strikes on Russian territory and the release of €90 bn to fund the war. In a public speech, she brazenly announced that the European Union was explicitly allied with Ukraine as a participant in the war. “In Europe, we already have huge technological and industrial capacity that can be deployed. And we have safe and secure production sites that can help to scale up,” von der Leyen said. She added: “But we do not have that battle-tested knowledge and expertise that Ukraine has forged. So the point I am making is that we need to combine our strengths. Together, we can work on joint production.” The innovation, which von der Leyen and her NATO planners presumably think is ever-so smart, is that European countries will produce munitions that are “safe” from Russian retaliatory strikes. The drones and missiles will be transported to Ukraine, from where they can be launched to hit deep into Russian territory to target the capital, Moscow, and take out oil industrial facilities all over the country. In recent months, NATO-backed Ukrainian drones have killed hundreds of Russian civilians. Von der Leyen’s pact with Ukraine is giving a green light to intensify this terror campaign. But it’s von der Leyen’s arrogant sense of impunity that is astounding and should be viewed as deeply disturbing. She apparently assumes that European countries can serve as an arsenal of weapons for Ukraine to attack Russia. Yet, in her view, Russia is not permitted to take retaliatory action against the EU (and NATO) as a participant in the war. This is insanely reckless and stupid. Of course, the European Union, NATO, and the United States have been participants in the proxy war against Russia since their coup in Kiev in 2014 to install and weaponize a NeoNazi regime. That background of weaponization and ideological programming culminated in war erupting in 2022. The Collective West has supplied an estimated $400 billion to prop up the regime since 2022, has supplied NATO mercenaries and advisors, as well as providing targeting logistics and operational expertise for long-range missiles. Ukraine has also signed bilateral military pacts with individual EU countries, such as Germany, France, Denmark, the Netherlands, and Norway. Britain has signed a One Hundred Year Defense Pact. All of these arrangements provide weapons, warplanes, drones, and missiles. However, the EU support that von der Leyen unveiled this week is a commitment from the entire 27-nation EU bloc to join the war on an industrial scale. In April this year, the Russian Ministry of Defense published a list of European companies and their locations that it said were partners in Ukraine’s war effort. Russia said that these sites could henceforth be considered legitimate targets for retaliation. So far, Russia has refrained from directly striking any EU or NATO state. But how long this Russian restraint will last is a pressing question in the light of increasing provocation by the EU and NATO. Von der Leyen has taken to describing the Ukrainian regime as a “net provider of security for Europe.” This is to supposedly justify European citizens enduring economic hardship as public monies are funnelled to the corrupt regime. This week, on cue for the drone deal and fusing of the EU and Ukrainian economies, Zelensky reshuffled his Cabinet for the second time in only a matter of months in an attempt to give the impression that he was cleaning up notorious corruption. Prime Minister Yulia Syrvydenko was sacked because she was close to Zelensky’s former Chief of Staff Andriy Yermak, who was forced to quit owing to a massive corruption scandal involving military procurement. In his latest clean-up act, Zelensky made the mistake of sacking Defense Minister Myhailo Federov, sparking public protests in Kiev and other cities. Federov, who had only been in the job for six months, was seen as an earnest reformer. His efforts riled military commanders, such as Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi, who want the graft gravy train to continue running. No surprise that Zelensky backed the old guard to stay in place, especially as the €90 bn EU freight car is soon to arrive in Kiev. Indeed, the tide is turning, but not in the way that delusional von der Leyen imagines. Russian forces on the ground are wearing down the last NATO-backed Ukrainian defenses while ramped-up Russian air strikes are destroying drone-making centers in Kiev and other cities. That is why the EU and NATO are innovating a new tactic of removing drone production sites from Ukraine and seemingly out of Russia’s reach by stationing these sites in European nations. The implicit idea is that the EU can scale up the war effort safely without suffering any consequences. The Russophobic EU leadership is driving Europe to war against Russia, as Professor Richard Sakwa commented this week in an interview with Glenn Diesen. The decisions are being made autocratically without any democratic mandate. Effectively, the EU has become a dictatorship for war. Another manifestation of this was seen at the Bastille Day parade in Paris on July 14, when NATO troops joined French and Ukrainian regiments in a unified show of belligerence towards Russia. And brain-dead bureaucrats like Ursula von der Leyen on her €400,ooo a-year salary are happily painting target signs across European nations. This is not sleepwalking into war. The European elites’ eyes are wide open and crazed. July 17, 2026 | Editorial Von der Leyen paints target signs on Europe with Ukraine drone pact This is not sleepwalking into war.
